title: tetragonia vestita
intro: Tetragonia vestita, also known as the New Zealand spinach or kōkihi, is a type of edible plant that belongs to the Aizoaceae family. It has been used for centuries in traditional medicine, particularly in Maori culture, for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Tetragonia vestita include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting digestive well-being. The plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or capsule, and is also consumed raw as a leafy green vegetable, similar to spinach.
